b110.title: Consciousness functions
b110.description: General mental functions of the state of awareness and alertness, including the clarity and continuity of the wakeful state.
inclusions: Functions of the state, continuity and quality of consciousness
Loss of consciousness, coma, vegetative states
Fugue, trance states, possession states
Drug-induced altered consciousness, delirium, stupor

b1100.title: State of consciousness
b1100.description: Mental functions that when disturbed produce states such as clouding of consciousness, stupor or coma.

b1101.title: Continuity of consciousness
b1101.description: Mental functions that produce sustained wakefulness, alertness and awareness and when disturbed may produce fugue, trance or other similar states.

b1102.title: Quality of consciousness
b1102.description: Mental functions that when altered affect changes in the quality of conscious awareness, such as drug-induced states of consciousness or delirium.

b114.title: Orientation functions
b114.description: General mental functions of knowing and ascertaining one's relation to self, to others, to time and to one's surroundings.
inclusions: Functions of orientation to time, place and person
Functions of orientation to self and others
Disorientation to time, place and person

b1140.title: Orientation to time
b1140.description: Mental functions that produce awareness of day, date, month and year.

b1141.title: Orientation to place
b1141.description: Mental functions that produce awareness of one's location, such as one's immediate surroundings, one's town or country.

b1142.title: Orientation to person
b1142.description: Mental functions that produce awareness of one's own identity and of individuals in one's immediate environment.

b11420.title: Orientation to self
b11420.description: Mental functions that produce awareness of one's own identity.

b11421.title: Orientation to others
b11421.description: Mental functions that produce awareness of the identity of other individuals in one's immediate environment.

b117.title: Intellectual functions
b117.description: General mental functions, required to understand and constructively integrate the various mental functions, including all cognitive functions and their development over the life span.
inclusions: Functions of intellectual growth
Intellectual disability, mental retardation, dementia

b122.title: Global psychosocial functions
b122.description: General mental functions, as they develop over the life span, required to understand and constructively integrate the mental functions that lead to the formation of the interpersonal skills needed to establish reciprocal social interactions, in terms of both meaning and purpose.
inclusions: Conditions such as autism

b126.title: Temperament and personality functions
b126.description: General mental functions of constitutional disposition of the individual to react in a particular way to situations, including the set of mental characteristics that makes the individual distinct from others.
inclusions: Functions of extraversion, introversion, agreeableness, conscientiousness, psychic and emotional stability, and openness to experience
Optimism, novelty seeking, confidence, trustworthiness

b1260.title: Extraversion
b1260.description: Mental functions that produce a personal disposition that is outgoing, sociable and demonstrative, as contrasted to being shy, restricted and inhibited.

b1261.title: Agreeableness
b1261.description: Mental functions that produce a personal disposition that is cooperative, amicable and accommodating, as contrasted to being unfriendly, opposing and defiant.

b1262.title: Conscientiousness
b1262.description: Mental functions that produce a personal disposition such as in being hard-working, methodical and scrupulous, as contrasted to being lazy, unreliable and irresponsible.

b1263.title: Psychic stability
b1263.description: Mental functions that produce a personal disposition that is even-tempered, calm and composed, as contrasted to being irritable, worried, erratic and moody.

b1264.title: Openness to experience
b1264.description: Mental functions that produce a personal disposition that is curious, imaginative, inquisitive and experience-seeking, as contrasted to being stagnant, inattentive and emotionally inexpressive.

b1265.title: Optimism
b1265.description: Mental functions that produce a personal disposition that is cheerful, buoyant and hopeful, as contrasted to being downhearted, gloomy and despairing.

b1266.title: Confidence
b1266.description: Mental functions that produce a personal disposition that is self-assured, bold and assertive, as contrasted to being timid, insecure and self-effacing.

b1267.title: Trustworthiness
b1267.description: Mental functions that produce a personal disposition that is dependable and principled, as contrasted to being deceitful and antisocially disposed.

b1268.title: Temperament and personality functions, other specified

b130.title: Energy and drive functions
b130.description: General mental functions of physiological and psychological mechanisms that cause the individual to move towards satisfying specific needs and general goals in a persistent manner.
inclusions: Functions of energy level, motivation, appetite, craving (including craving for substances that can be abused), and impulse control

b1300.title: Energy level
b1300.description: Mental functions that produce vigour and stamina.

b1301.title: Motivation
b1301.description: Mental functions that produce the incentive to act; the conscious or unconscious driving force for action.

b1302.title: Appetite
b1302.description: Mental functions that produce a natural longing or desire, especially the natural and recurring desire for food and drink.

b1303.title: Craving
b1303.description: Mental functions that produce the urge to consume substances, including substances that can be abused.

b1304.title: Impulse control
b1304.description: Mental functions that regulate and resist sudden intense urges to do something.

b1308.title: Energy and drive functions, other specified

b134.title: Sleep functions
b134.description: General mental functions of periodic, reversible and selective physical and mental disengagement from one's immediate environment accompanied by characteristic physiological changes.
inclusions: Functions of amount of sleep, onset of sleep, maintenance of sleep, quality of sleep
Functions involving the sleep cycle, such as in insomnia, hypersomnia and narcolepsy

b1340.title: Amount of sleep
b1340.description: Mental functions involved in the time spent in the state of sleep in the diurnal cycle or circadian rhythm.

b1341.title: Onset of sleep
b1341.description: Mental functions that produce the transition between wakefulness and sleep.

b1342.title: Maintenance of sleep
b1342.description: Mental functions that sustain the state of being asleep.

b1343.title: Quality of sleep
b1343.description: Mental functions that produce the natural sleep leading to optimal physical and mental rest and relaxation.

b1344.title: Functions involving the sleep cycle
b1344.description: Mental functions that produce rapid eye movement (REM) sleep and non-REM sleep associated with dreaming, the sleep cycle and its deviation.

b139.title: Global mental functions, other specified and unspecified

b140.title: Attention functions
b140.description: Specific mental functions of focusing on an external stimulus or internal experience for the required period of time.
inclusions: Functions of sustaining attention, shifting attention, dividing attention, sharing attention
Concentration, distractibility

b1400.title: Sustaining attention
b1400.description: Mental functions that produce concentration for the period of time required.

b1401.title: Shifting attention
b1401.description: Mental functions that permit refocusing concentration from one stimulus to another.

b1402.title: Dividing attention
b1402.description: Mental functions that permit focusing on two or more stimuli at the same time.

b1403.title: Sharing attention
b1403.description: Mental functions that permit focusing on the same stimulus by two or more people.

b144.title: Memory functions
b144.description: Specific mental functions of registering and storing information and retrieving it as needed.
inclusions: Functions of short-term and long-term memory, immediate, recent and remote memory
Memory span, retrieval of memory; remembering
Functions used in recalling and learning, such as in nominal, selective and dissociative amnesia

b1440.title: Short-term memory
b1440.description: Mental functions that produce a temporary, disruptable memory store of around 30 seconds duration from which information is lost if not consolidated into long-term memory.

b1441.title: Long-term memory
b1441.description: Mental functions that produce a memory system permitting the long-term storage of information from short-term memory, both autobiographic memory for past events and semantic memory for language and facts.

b1442.title: Retrieval of memory
b1442.description: Specific mental functions of recalling information stored in long-term memory and bringing it into awareness.

b147.title: Psychomotor functions
b147.description: Specific mental functions of control over both motor and psychological events at the body level.
inclusions: Functions of psychomotor control, such as in psychomotor retardation, excitement, agitation, posturing, catatonia, negativism, ambitendency, echopraxia and echolalia
Quality of psychomotor function
exclusions: Mental function of sequencing complex movements (b176)

b1470.title: Psychomotor control
b1470.description: Mental functions that regulate speed of behaviour or response time that involves both motor and psychological components, such as disruptions in control producing psychomotor retardation or psychomotor excitement.

b1471.title: Quality of psychomotor functions
b1471.description: Mental functions that produce non-verbal behaviour in the proper sequence and character of its sub-components, such as hand and eye coordination, or gait.

b152.title: Emotional functions
b152.description: Specific mental functions related to the feeling and affective components of the processes of the mind.
inclusions: Functions of appropriateness of emotion, regulation and range of emotion; affect
Sadness, happiness, love, fear, anger, hate, tension, anxiety, joy, sorrow
Lability of emotion; flattening of affect

b1520.title: Appropriateness of emotion
b1520.description: Mental functions that produce congruence of feeling or affect with the situation, such as happiness at receiving good news.

b1521.title: Regulation of emotion
b1521.description: Mental functions that control the experience and display of affect.

b1522.title: Range of emotion
b1522.description: Mental functions that produce the spectrum of experience of arousal of affect or feelings such as love, hate, anxiousness, sorrow, joy, fear and anger.

b156.title: Perceptual functions
b156.description: Specific mental functions of recognizing and interpreting sensory stimuli.
inclusions: Functions of auditory, visual, olfactory, gustatory, tactile and visuospatial perception, such as hallucination or illusion

b1560.title: Auditory perception
b1560.description: Mental functions involved in discriminating sounds, tones, pitches and other acoustic stimuli.

b1561.title: Visual perception
b1561.description: Mental functions involved in discriminating shape, size, colour and other ocular stimuli.

b1562.title: Olfactory perception
b1562.description: Mental functions involved in distinguishing differences in smells.

b1563.title: Gustatory perception
b1563.description: Mental functions involved in distinguishing differences in tastes, such as sweet, sour, salty and bitter stimuli, detected by the tongue.

b1564.title: Tactile perception
b1564.description: Mental functions involved in distinguishing differences in texture, such as rough or smooth stimuli, detected by touch.

b1565.title: Visuospatial perception
b1565.description: Mental function involved in distinguishing by sight the relative position of objects in the environment or in relation to oneself.

b160.title: Thought functions
b160.description: Specific mental functions related to the ideational component of the mind.
inclusions: Functions of pace, form, control and content of thought
Goal-directed and non-goal-directed thought functions
Logically coherent thought functions, such as pressured thinking, flight of ideas, thought block, incoherence of thought, tangentiality, circumstantiality, delusions, obsessions and compulsions

b1600.title: Pace of thought
b1600.description: Mental functions that govern speed of the thinking process.

b1601.title: Form of thought
b1601.description: Mental functions that organize the thinking process as to its coherence and logic.
inclusions: Ideational perseveration, tangentiality, circumstantiality

b1602.title: Content of thought
b1602.description: Mental functions consisting of the ideas that are present in the thinking process and what is being conceptualized.
inclusions: Delusions, overvalued ideas, somatization

b1603.title: Control of thought
b1603.description: Mental functions that provide volitional control of thinking and are recognized as such by the person.
inclusions: Rumination, obsessions, thought broadcast, thought insertion and thought withdrawal

b164.title: Higher-level cognitive functions
b164.description: Specific mental functions especially dependent on the frontal lobes of the brain, including complex goal-directed behaviours such as decision-making, abstract thinking, planning and carrying out plans, mental flexibility, and deciding which behaviours are appropriate under what circumstances; often called executive or frontal lobe functions.
inclusions: Functions of abstraction and organization of ideas; time management, insight and judgement
Concept formation, categorization and cognitive flexibility

b1640.title: Abstraction
b1640.description: Mental functions of creating general ideas, qualities or characteristics out of, and distinct from, concrete realities, specific objects or actual instances.

b1641.title: Organization and planning
b1641.description: Mental functions of coordinating parts into a whole, of systematizing; the mental function involved in developing a method of proceeding or acting.

b1642.title: Time management
b1642.description: Mental functions of ordering events in a chronological sequence, allocating amounts of time to events and activities.

b1643.title: Cognitive flexibility
b1643.description: Mental functions of changing strategies, or shifting mental sets, especially as involved in problem-solving.

b1644.title: Insight
b1644.description: Mental functions of awareness and understanding of oneself and one's behaviour.

b1645.title: Judgement
b1645.description: Mental functions involved in discriminating between and evaluating different options, such as those involved in forming an opinion.

b1646.title: Problem-solving
b1646.description: Mental functions of identifying, analysing and integrating incongruent or conflicting information into a solution.

b167.title: Mental functions of language
b167.description: Specific mental functions of recognizing and using signs, symbols and other components of a language.
inclusions: Functions of reception and deciphering of spoken, written or other forms of language such as sign language
Functions of expression of spoken, written or other forms of language
Integrative language functions, spoken and written, such as involved in receptive, expressive, Broca's, Wernicke's and conduction aphasia
exclusions: Mental function of sequencing complex movements (b176)

b1670.title: Reception of language
b1670.description: Specific mental functions of decoding messages in spoken, written or other forms, such as sign language, to obtain their meaning.

b16700.title: Reception of spoken language
b16700.description: Mental functions of decoding spoken messages to obtain their meaning.

b16701.title: Reception of written language
b16701.description: Mental functions of decoding written messages to obtain their meaning.

b16702.title: Reception of sign language
b16702.description: Mental functions of decoding messages in languages that use signs made by hands and other movements, to obtain their meaning.

b1671.title: Expression of language
b1671.description: Specific mental functions necessary to produce meaningful messages in spoken, written, signed or other forms of language.

b16710.title: Expression of spoken language
b16710.description: Mental functions necessary to produce meaningful spoken messages.

b16711.title: Expression of written language
b16711.description: Mental functions necessary to produce meaningful written messages.

b16712.title: Expression of sign language
b16712.description: Mental functions necessary to produce meaningful messages in languages that use signs made by hands and other movements.

b1672.title: Integrative language functions
b1672.description: Mental functions that organize semantic and symbolic meaning, grammatical structure and ideas for the production of messages in spoken, written or other forms of language.

b1678.title: Mental functions of language, other specified

b172.title: Calculation functions
b172.description: Specific mental functions of determination, approximation and manipulation of mathematical symbols and processes.
inclusions: Functions of addition, subtraction, and other simple mathematical calculations
Functions of complex mathematical operations

b1720.title: Simple calculation
b1720.description: Mental functions of computing with numbers, such as addition, subtraction, multiplication and division.

b1721.title: Complex calculation
b1721.description: Mental functions of translating word problems into arithmetical procedures, translating mathematical formulae into arithmetical procedures, and other complex manipulations involving numbers.

b176.title: Mental function of sequencing complex movements
b176.description: Specific mental functions of sequencing and coordinating complex, purposeful movements.
inclusions: Ideational and ideomotor apraxia

b180.title: Experience of self and time functions
b180.description: Specific mental functions related to the awareness of one's identity, one's body, one's position in the reality of one's environment and of time.
inclusions: Functions of experience of self, body image and time

b1800.title: Experience of self
b1800.description: Specific mental functions of being aware of one's own identity and one's position in the reality of the environment around oneself.
inclusions: Depersonalization, derealization

b1801.title: Body image
b1801.description: Specific mental functions related to the representation and awareness of one's body.
inclusions: Body dysmorphia

b1802.title: Experience of time
b1802.description: Specific mental functions of the subjective experiences related to the length and passage of time.
inclusions: Jamais vu, deja vu

b1808.title: Experience of self and time functions, other specified

b1809.title: Experience of self and time functions, unspecified

b189.title: Specific mental functions, other specified and unspecified

b210.title: Seeing functions
b210.description: Sensory functions relating to sensing the presence of light and sensing the form, size, shape and colour of the visual stimuli.
inclusions: Visual acuity functions; visual field functions; quality of vision
Functions of sensing light and colour, visual acuity of distant and near vision, monocular and binocular vision; visual picture quality
Impairments such as myopia, hypermetropia, astigmatism, hemianopia, colour-blindness, tunnel vision, central and peripheral scotoma, diplopia, night blindness and adaptability to light

b2100.title: Visual acuity functions
b2100.description: Seeing functions of sensing form and contour, both binocular and monocular, for both distant and near vision.

b21000.title: Binocular acuity of distant vision
b21000.description: Seeing functions of sensing size, form and contour, using both eyes, for objects distant from the eye.

b21001.title: Monocular acuity of distant vision
b21001.description: Seeing functions of sensing size, form and contour, using either right or left eye alone, for objects distant from the eye.

b21002.title: Binocular acuity of near vision
b21002.description: Seeing functions of sensing size, form and contour, using both eyes, for objects close to the eye.

b21003.title: Monocular acuity of near vision
b21003.description: Seeing functions of sensing size, form and contour, using either right or left eye alone, for objects close to the eye.

b2101.title: Visual field functions
b2101.description: Seeing functions related to the entire area that can be seen with fixation of gaze.
inclusions: Scotoma

b2102.title: Quality of vision
b2102.description: Seeing functions involving light sensitivity, colour vision, contrast sensitivity, and the overall quality of the picture.

b21020.title: Light sensitivity
b21020.description: Seeing functions of sensing a minimum amount of light (light minimum), and the minimum difference in intensity (light difference).
inclusions: Functions of dark adaptation

b21021.title: Colour vision
b21021.description: Seeing functions of differentiating and matching colours.

b21022.title: Contrast sensitivity
b21022.description: Seeing functions of separating figure from ground, involving the minimum amount of luminance required.

b21023.title: Visual picture quality
b21023.description: Seeing functions involving the quality of the image.
inclusions: Strabismus, diplopia, floaters, distortion of picture

b215.title: Functions of structures adjoining the eye
b215.description: Functions of structures in and around the eye that facilitate seeing functions.
inclusions: Functions of internal muscles of the eye, eyelid, external muscles of the eye, including voluntary and tracking movements and fixation of the eye, lachrymal glands, accommodation, pupillary reflex
Nystagmus, xerophthalmia and ptosis

b2150.title: Functions of internal muscles of the eye
b2150.description: Functions of the muscles inside the eye, such as the iris, that adjust the shape and size of the pupil and of the lens of the eye.
inclusions: Functions of accommodation, pupillary reflex

b2151.title: Functions of the eyelid
b2151.description: Functions of the eyelid, such as the protective reflex of closure of the eyelid.

b2152.title: Functions of external muscles of the eye
b2152.description: Functions of the muscles that are used to look in different directions, to follow an object as it moves across the visual field, to produce saccadic jumps, and to fixate the eye.
inclusions: Nystagmus, strabismus-related cooperation of both eyes

b2153.title: Functions of lachrymal glands
b2153.description: Functions of the tear glands and ducts.

b2158.title: Functions of structures adjoining the eye, other specified

b2159.title: Functions of structures adjoining the eye, unspecified

b220.title: Sensations associated with the eye and adjoining structures
b220.description: Sensations of tired, dry and itching eye and related feelings.
inclusions: Feelings of pressure behind the eye, of something in the eye, eye strain, burning in the eye; irritation in the eye

b229.title: Seeing and related functions, other specified and unspecified

b230.title: Hearing functions
b230.description: Sensory functions relating to sensing the presence of sounds and discriminating the location, pitch, loudness and quality of sounds.
inclusions: Functions of hearing, auditory discrimination, localization of sound source, lateralization of sound, speech discrimination
Impairments such as deafness, hearing impairment and hearing loss

b2300.title: Sound detection
b2300.description: Sensory functions relating to sensing the presence of sounds.

b2301.title: Sound discrimination
b2301.description: Sensory functions relating to sensing the difference between sounds.

b2302.title: Localization of sound source
b2302.description: Sensory functions relating to determining the location of the source of sound.

b2303.title: Lateralization of sound
b2303.description: Sensory functions relating to determining whether the sound is coming from the right or the left side.

b2304.title: Speech discrimination
b2304.description: Sensory functions relating to determining spoken language and distinguishing it from other sounds.

b235.title: Vestibular functions
b235.description: Sensory functions of the inner ear related to position, balance and movement.
inclusions: Functions of position and positional sense; functions of balance of the body and movement
exclusions: Sensations associated with hearing and vestibular function (b240)

b2350.title: Vestibular function of position
b2350.description: Sensory functions of the inner ear related to determining the position of the body.

b2351.title: Vestibular function of balance
b2351.description: Sensory functions of the inner ear related to determining the balance of the body.

b2352.title: Vestibular function of movement
b2352.description: Sensory functions of the inner ear related to determining movement of the body, including its direction and speed.

b240.title: Sensations associated with hearing and vestibular function
b240.description: Sensations of dizziness, falling, tinnitus and vertigo.
inclusions: Sensations of ringing in ears, irritation in the ear, aural pressure, nausea associated with dizziness or vertigo

b2400.title: Ringing in ears or tinnitus
b2400.description: Sensation of low-pitched rushing, hissing or ringing in the ear.

b2401.title: Dizziness
b2401.description: Sensation of motion involving either oneself or one's environment; sensation of rotating, swaying or tilting.

b2402.title: Sensation of falling
b2402.description: Sensation of losing one's grip and dropping.

b2403.title: Nausea associated with dizziness or vertigo
b2403.description: Sensation of wanting to vomit elicited by dizziness or vertigo.

b2404.title: Irritation in the ear
b2404.description: Sensation of itching or other similar sensations in the ear.

b2405.title: Aural pressure
b2405.description: Sensation of pressure in the ear.

b2408.title: Sensations associated with hearing and vestibular function, other specified

b2409.title: Sensations associated with hearing and vestibular function, unspecified

b249.title: Hearing and vestibular functions, other specified and unspecified

b250.title: Taste function
b250.description: Sensory functions of sensing qualities of bitterness, sweetness, sourness and saltiness.
inclusions: Functions of sense of taste; impairments such as ageusia and hypogeusia

b255.title: Smell function
b255.description: Sensory functions of sensing odours and smells.
inclusions: Functions of sense of smell; impairments such as anosmia and hyposmia

b260.title: Proprioceptive function
b260.description: Sensory functions of sensing the relative position of body parts.
inclusions: Functions of position sense and kinaesthetic sense
exclusions: Sensations related to muscles and movement functions (b780)

b265.title: Touch function
b265.description: Sensory functions of sensing surfaces and their texture or quality.
inclusions: Functions of touching, feeling of touch; impairments such as numbness, anaesthesia, tingling, paraesthesia and hyperaesthesia
exclusions: Sensory functions related to temperature and other stimuli (b270)

b270.title: Sensory functions related to temperature and other stimuli
b270.description: Sensory functions of sensing temperature, vibration, pressure and noxious stimulus.
inclusions: Functions of being sensitive to temperature, vibration, shaking or oscillation, superficial pressure, deep pressure, burning sensation or a noxious stimulus

b2700.title: Sensitivity to temperature
b2700.description: Sensory functions of sensing cold and heat.

b2701.title: Sensitivity to vibration
b2701.description: Sensory functions of sensing shaking or oscillation.

b2702.title: Sensitivity to pressure
b2702.description: Sensory functions of sensing pressure against or on the surface of the skin.

b2703.title: Sensitivity to a noxious stimulus
b2703.description: Sensory functions of sensing a painful or uncomfortable sensation.

b2708.title: Sensory functions related to temperature and other stimuli, other specified

b2709.title: Sensory functions related to temperature and other stimuli, unspecified

b279.title: Additional sensory functions, other specified and unspecified

b280.title: Sensation of pain
b280.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling indicating potential or actual damage to some body structure.
inclusions: Sensations of generalized or localized pain, in one or more body part, pain in a dermatome, stabbing pain, burning pain, dull pain, aching pain; impairments such as myalgia, analgesia and hyperalgesia

b2800.title: Generalized pain
b2800.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling indicating potential or actual damage to some body structure felt all over, or throughout the body.

b2801.title: Pain in body part
b2801.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling indicating potential or actual damage to some body structure felt in a specific part or parts of the body.

b28010.title: Pain in head and neck
b28010.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in the head and neck indicating potential or actual damage to some body structure.

b28011.title: Pain in chest
b28011.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in the chest indicating potential or actual damage to some body structure.

b28012.title: Pain in stomach or abdomen
b28012.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in the stomach or abdomen indicating potential or actual damage to some body structure.

b28013.title: Pain in back
b28013.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in the back indicating potential or actual damage to some body structure.

b28014.title: Pain in upper limb
b28014.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in one or both upper limbs, including hands.

b28015.title: Pain in lower limb
b28015.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in one or both lower limbs, including feet.

b28016.title: Pain in joints
b28016.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in one or more joints.

b28018.title: Pain in body part, other specified

b2802.title: Pain in multiple body parts
b2802.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in two or more body parts.

b2803.title: Radiating pain in a dermatome
b2803.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling throughout a dermatome.

b2804.title: Radiating pain in a segment or region
b2804.description: Sensation of unpleasant feeling in areas of skin, not corresponding to a specific dermatome.

b289.title: Sensation of pain, other specified and unspecified

b298.title: Sensory functions and pain, other specified

b310.title: Voice functions
b310.description: Functions of the production and quality of voice.
inclusions: Functions of production and quality of voice; phonation, pitch, loudness and other qualities of voice
Impairments such as aphonia, dysphonia, hoarseness, hypernasality and hyponasality

b3100.title: Production of voice
b3100.description: Functions of the production of sound made through the coordination of the larynx and surrounding muscles with the respiratory system.
inclusions: Functions of phonation, loudness; impairments such as aphonia, dysphonia, hoarseness

b3101.title: Quality of voice
b3101.description: Functions of the production of characteristics of voice including pitch, resonance and other features.
inclusions: Functions of high or low pitch, loudness; impairments such as hypernasality, hyponasality, dysphonia, hoarseness or harshness of voice

b320.title: Articulation functions
b320.description: Functions of the production of speech sounds.
inclusions: Functions of enunciation, articulation of phonemes; spastic, ataxic, flaccid dysarthria; anarthria

b330.title: Fluency and rhythm of speech functions
b330.description: Functions of the production of flow and tempo of speech.
inclusions: Functions of fluency, rhythm, speed and melody of speech; prosody and intonation; impairments such as stuttering, stammering, bradylalia, tachylalia, dysfluency, dysrhythmia and dysprosodia

b3300.title: Fluency of speech
b3300.description: Functions of the production of smooth, uninterrupted flow of speech.
inclusions: Impairments such as stuttering, stammering, cluttering, dysfluency

b3301.title: Rhythm of speech
b3301.description: Functions of the modulated, tempo-governed pattern of speech.
inclusions: Impairments such as dysrhythmia of speech

b3302.title: Speed of speech
b3302.description: Functions of the rate of speech production.
inclusions: Impairments such as bradylalia and tachylalia

b3303.title: Melody of speech
b3303.description: Functions of the modulation of pitch patterns in speech.
inclusions: Prosody, intonation, melody; impairments such as monotonous speech, dysprosodia

b3308.title: Fluency and rhythm of speech functions, other specified

b3309.title: Fluency and rhythm of speech functions, unspecified

b340.title: Alternative vocalization functions
b340.description: Functions of the production of other manners of vocalization.
inclusions: Functions of the production of tones, making a range of sounds; crying, yelling and screaming; singing; humming; babbling
exclusions: Fluency and rhythm of speech functions (b330)

b3400.title: Production of notes
b3400.description: Functions of the production of musical vocal sounds.
inclusions: Sustaining, modulating and terminating production of single tones with the voice, as in singing, chanting, babbling

b3401.title: Making a range of sounds
b3401.description: Functions of the production of a wide variety of vocalizations.
inclusions: Making sounds such as humming, chanting, babbling, singing, crying, screaming, yelling

b398.title: Voice and speech functions, other specified

b410.title: Heart functions
b410.description: Functions of pumping the blood in adequate or required amounts and pressure throughout the body.
inclusions: Functions of heart rate, rhythm and output; contraction force of ventricular muscles; functions of heart valves; pumping the blood through the pulmonary circuit; dynamics of circulation to the heart
Impairments such as tachycardia, bradycardia and irregular heart beat, as in heart failure, cardiomyopathy, myocarditis and coronary insufficiency

b4100.title: Heart rate
b4100.description: Functions related to the number of times the heart contracts per minute.
inclusions: Impairments such as rates that are too fast (tachycardia) or too slow (bradycardia)

b4101.title: Heart rhythm
b4101.description: Functions related to the regularity of the beating of the heart.
inclusions: Impairments such as arrhythmias

b4102.title: Contraction force of ventricular muscles
b4102.description: Functions related to the amount of blood pumped by the ventricular muscles during every beat.
inclusions: Impairments such as decreased cardiac output

b4103.title: Blood supply to the heart
b4103.description: Functions related to the volume of blood available to the heart muscle.
inclusions: Impairments such as coronary ischaemia

b415.title: Blood vessel functions
b415.description: Functions of transporting blood throughout the body.
inclusions: Functions of arteries, capillaries and veins; vasomotor function; functions of pulmonary arteries, capillaries and veins
Impairments such as blockage or constriction of arteries; atherosclerosis, arteriosclerosis, thromboembolism and varicose veins

b4150.title: Functions of arteries
b4150.description: Functions related to blood flow in the arteries.
inclusions: Impairments such as arterial dilation or constriction; atherosclerosis, arteriosclerosis, thromboembolism

b4151.title: Functions of capillaries
b4151.description: Functions related to blood flow in the capillaries.

b4152.title: Functions of veins
b4152.description: Functions related to blood flow in the veins.
inclusions: Impairments such as venous dilation; venous constriction; venous insufficiency, as in varicose veins

b420.title: Blood pressure functions
b420.description: Functions of maintaining the pressure of blood within the arteries.
inclusions: Functions of maintenance of blood pressure; increased and decreased blood pressure
Impairments such as hypotension, hypertension and postural hypotension

b4200.title: Increased blood pressure
b4200.description: Functions related to a rise in systolic or diastolic blood pressure above normal for the age.

b4201.title: Decreased blood pressure
b4201.description: Functions related to a fall in systolic or diastolic blood pressure below normal for the age.

b4202.title: Maintenance of blood pressure
b4202.description: Functions related to maintaining an appropriate blood pressure in response to changes in the body.

b429.title: Functions of the cardiovascular system, other specified and unspecified

b430.title: Haematological system functions
b430.description: Functions of blood production, oxygen and metabolite carriage, and clotting.
inclusions: Functions of the production of blood and bone marrow; oxygen-carrying functions of blood; blood-related functions of spleen; metabolite-carrying functions of blood; clotting; impairments such as anaemia, haemophilia and other clotting dysfunctions

b4300.title: Production of blood
b4300.description: Functions related to the production of blood.

b4301.title: Oxygen-carrying functions of the blood
b4301.description: Functions related to the blood's capacity to carry oxygen throughout the body.

b4302.title: Metabolic function of the blood
b4302.description: Functions related to the blood's capacity to carry metabolites throughout the body.

b4303.title: Clotting functions
b4303.description: Functions related to the coagulation of blood, such as at a site of injury.
inclusions: Impairments such as haemophilia, thrombocytopenia, prothrombinaemia and other clotting dysfunctions

b435.title: Immunological system functions
b435.description: Functions of the body related to protection against foreign substances, including infections, by specific and non-specific immune responses.
inclusions: Immune response (specific and non-specific); hypersensitivity reactions; functions of lymphatic vessels and nodes; functions of cell-mediated immunity, antibody-mediated immunity; response to immunization
Impairments such as autoimmunity, allergic reactions, lymphadenitis, lymphoedema

b4350.title: Immune response
b4350.description: Functions of the body's response of sensitization to foreign substances, including infections.

b43500.title: Specific immune response
b43500.description: Functions of the body's response of sensitization to a specific foreign substance.

b43501.title: Non-specific immune response
b43501.description: Functions of the body's general response of sensitization to foreign substances, including infections.

b4351.title: Hypersensitivity reactions
b4351.description: Functions of the body's response of increased sensitization to foreign substances, such as in sensitivities to different antigens.
inclusions: Impairments such as hypersensitivities or allergies

b4352.title: Functions of lymphatic vessels
b4352.description: Functions related to vascular channels that transport lymph.

b4353.title: Functions of lymph nodes
b4353.description: Functions related to glands along the course of lymphatic vessels.

b439.title: Functions of the haematological and immunological systems, other specified and unspecified

b440.title: Respiration functions
b440.description: Functions of inhaling air into the lungs, the exchange of gases between air and blood, and exhaling air.
inclusions: Functions of respiration rate, rhythm and depth; impairments such as apnoea, hyperventilation, irregular respiration, paradoxical respiration, bronchial spasm and pulmonary emphysema

b4400.title: Respiration rate
b4400.description: Functions related to the number of breaths taken per minute.
inclusions: Impairments such as rates that are too fast (tachypnoea) or too slow (bradypnoea)

b4401.title: Respiratory rhythm
b4401.description: Functions related to the periodicity and regularity of breathing.
inclusions: Impairments such as irregular breathing

b4402.title: Depth of respiration
b4402.description: Functions related to the volume of expansion of the lungs during breathing.
inclusions: Impairments such as superficial or shallow respiration

b445.title: Respiratory muscle functions
b445.description: Functions of the muscles involved in breathing.
inclusions: Functions of thoracic respiratory muscles; functions of the diaphragm; functions of accessory respiratory muscles

b4450.title: Functions of the thoracic respiratory muscles
b4450.description: Functions of the thoracic muscles involved in breathing.

b4451.title: Functions of the diaphragm
b4451.description: Functions of the diaphragm as involved in breathing.

b4452.title: Functions of accessory respiratory muscles
b4452.description: Functions of the additional muscles involved in breathing.

b449.title: Functions of the respiratory system, other specified and unspecified

b450.title: Additional respiratory functions
b450.description: Additional functions related to breathing, such as coughing, sneezing and yawning.
inclusions: Functions of blowing, whistling and mouth breathing

b455.title: Exercise tolerance functions
b455.description: Functions related to respiratory and cardiovascular capacity as required for enduring physical exertion.
inclusions: Functions of physical endurance, aerobic capacity, stamina and fatiguability
Impairments such as cardiac and respiratory insufficiency due to exertion, exercise intolerance

b4550.title: General physical endurance
b4550.description: Functions related to the general level of tolerance of physical exercise or stamina.

b4551.title: Aerobic capacity
b4551.description: Functions related to the extent to which a person can exercise without getting out of breath.

b4552.title: Fatiguability
b4552.description: Functions related to susceptibility to fatigue, at any level of exertion.

b460.title: Sensations associated with cardiovascular and respiratory functions
b460.description: Sensations such as missed beats, palpitation and shortness of breath.
inclusions: Sensations of tightness in chest, feelings of irregular beat, dyspnoea, air hunger, choking, gagging and wheezing

b469.title: Additional functions and sensations of the cardiovascular and respiratory systems, other specified and unspecified

b498.title: Functions of the cardiovascular, haematological, immunological and respiratory systems, other specified

b499.title: Functions of the cardiovascular, haematological, immunological and respiratory systems, unspecified

b510.title: Ingestion functions
b510.description: Functions related to taking in and manipulating solids or liquids through the mouth into the body.
inclusions: Functions of sucking, chewing and biting, manipulating food in the mouth, salivation, swallowing, burping, regurgitation, spitting and vomiting
Impairments such as dysphagia, aspiration of food, aerophagia, excessive salivation, drooling and insufficient salivation
exclusions: Sensations associated with the digestive system (b535)

b5100.title: Sucking
b5100.description: Functions of drawing into the mouth by a sucking force produced by movements of cheeks, lips and tongue.

b5101.title: Biting
b5101.description: Functions of cutting into and breaking up food with the front teeth.

b5102.title: Chewing
b5102.description: Functions of crushing, grinding and masticating food with the back teeth (e.g. molars).

b5103.title: Manipulation of food in the mouth
b5103.description: Functions of moving food around the mouth with the teeth and tongue.

b5104.title: Salivation
b5104.description: Function of the production of saliva within the mouth.

b5105.title: Swallowing
b5105.description: Functions of clearing the food and drink through the oral cavity, pharynx and oesophagus into the stomach at an appropriate rate and speed.
inclusions: Oral dysphagia, pharyngeal dysphagia, oesophageal dysphagia; impairments such as aspiration of food; aspiration

b51050.title: Oral swallowing
b51050.description: Function of clearing food and drink through the oral cavity at an appropriate rate and speed.

b51051.title: Pharyngeal swallowing
b51051.description: Function of clearing food and drink through the pharynx at an appropriate rate and speed.

b51052.title: Oesophageal swallowing
b51052.description: Function of clearing food and drink through the oesophagus at an appropriate rate and speed.

b5106.title: Regurgitation and vomiting
b5106.description: Functions of moving food or liquid in the reverse direction to ingestion, from the stomach to the oesophagus to the mouth and out.

b515.title: Digestive functions
b515.description: Functions of transporting food through the gastrointestinal tract, breakdown of food and absorption of nutrients.
inclusions: Functions of transport of food through the stomach, peristalsis; breakdown of food, enzyme production and action in stomach and intestines; absorption of nutrients and tolerance to food; impairments such as in hyperacidity of the stomach, malabsorption, intolerance to food, hypermotility of intestines, intestinal paralysis, intestinal obstruction and decreased bile production
exclusions: Sensations associated with the digestive system (b535)

b5150.title: Transport of food through stomach and intestines
b5150.description: Peristalsis and related functions that mechanically move food through the stomach and intestines.

b5151.title: Breakdown of food
b5151.description: Functions of mechanically and chemically reducing food to smaller particles in the gastrointestinal tract.

b5152.title: Absorption of nutrients
b5152.description: Functions of passing food constituents (nutrients) through the wall of the intestines into the blood stream.

b5153.title: Tolerance to food
b5153.description: Functions of accepting appropriate food and rejecting unsuitable food.

b520.title: Assimilation functions
b520.description: Functions by which nutrients are converted into components of the living body.
inclusions: Functions of storage of nutrients in the body

b525.title: Defecation functions
b525.description: Functions of elimination of wastes and undigested food as faeces and related functions.
inclusions: Functions of elimination, faecal consistency, frequency of defecation; faecal continence, flatulence
Impairments such as constipation, diarrhoea, watery stool and anal sphincter incompetence or incontinence
exclusions: Sensations associated with the digestive system (b535)

b5250.title: Elimination of faeces
b5250.description: Functions of the elimination of waste from the rectum, including the functions of contraction of the abdominal muscles in doing so.

b5251.title: Faecal consistency
b5251.description: Consistency of faeces such as hard, firm, soft or watery.

b5252.title: Frequency of defecation
b5252.description: Functions involved in the frequency of defecation.

b5253.title: Faecal continence
b5253.description: Functions involved in voluntary control over the defecation function.

b5254.title: Flatulence
b5254.description: Functions involved in the expulsion of excessive amounts of air or gases from the intestines.

b530.title: Weight maintenance functions
b530.description: Functions of maintaining appropriate body weight, including weight gain during the developmental period.
inclusions: Functions of maintenance of acceptable Body Mass Index (BMI); impairments such as underweight, cachexia, wasting, overweight, emaciation and such as in primary and secondary obesity

b535.title: Sensations associated with the digestive system
b535.description: Sensations arising from eating, drinking and related digestive functions.
inclusions: Sensations of nausea, feeling of bloating, and the sensation of abdominal cramp; feeling full, feeling of throat spasm, stomach spasm, gas in stomach, and heartburn

b5351.title: Feeling of bloating
b5351.description: Sensation of distension of the stomach or abdomen.

b5352.title: Sensation of abdominal cramp
b5352.description: Sensation of spasmodic or painful muscular contractions of the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract.

b5358.title: Sensations associated with the digestive system, other specified

b5359.title: Sensations associated with the digestive system, unspecified

b539.title: Functions related to the digestive system, other specified and unspecified

b540.title: General metabolic functions
b540.description: Functions of regulation of the essential components of the body such as carbohydrates, proteins, fats, through metabolism of food into energy.
inclusions: Functions of metabolism, basal metabolic rate, metabolism of carbohydrate, protein and fat, catabolism, anabolism, energy production in the body
Impairments such as metabolic acidosis and alkalosis, lipaemia, dehydration, hyperbilirubinaemia
exclusions: Water, mineral and electrolyte balance functions (b545)

b5400.title: Basal metabolic rate
b5400.description: Functions involved in oxygen consumption of the body at specified conditions of rest and temperature.
inclusions: Increase and decrease of metabolic rate

b5401.title: Carbohydrate metabolism
b5401.description: Functions involved in the process by which carbohydrates in the diet are stored and broken down into glucose and subsequently into carbon dioxide and water.

b5402.title: Protein metabolism
b5402.description: Functions involved in the process by which proteins in the diet are converted to amino acids and broken down further in the body.

b5403.title: Fat metabolism
b5403.description: Functions involved in the process by which fat in the diet is stored and broken down in the body.

b545.title: Water, mineral and electrolyte balance functions
b545.description: Functions of the regulation of water, minerals and electrolytes in the body.
inclusions: Functions of water balance; balance of minerals such as calcium, zinc and iron; balance of electrolytes such as sodium and potassium
Impairments such as in water retention, dehydration; hypercalcaemia, hypocalcaemia; iron deficiency, hypernatraemia, hyponatraemia, hyperkalaemia and hypokalaemia

b5450.title: Water balance
b5450.description: Functions involved in maintaining the level or amount of water in the body.

b54500.title: Water retention
b54500.description: Functions involved in keeping water in the body.

b54501.title: Maintenance of water balance
b54501.description: Functions involved in maintaining the optimal amount of water in the body.

b5451.title: Mineral balance
b5451.description: Functions involved in maintaining an equilibrium between intake, storage, utilization and excretion of minerals in the body.

b5452.title: Electrolyte balance
b5452.description: Functions involved in maintaining an equilibrium between intake, storage, utilization and excretion of electrolytes in the body.

b5458.title: Water, mineral and electrolyte balance functions, other specified

b5459.title: Water, mineral and electrolyte balance functions, unspecified

b550.title: Thermoregulatory functions
b550.description: Functions of the regulation of body temperature.
inclusions: Functions of maintenance of body temperature; impairments such as hypothermia, hyperthermia

b5500.title: Body temperature
b5500.description: Functions involved in regulating the core temperature of the body.
inclusions: Impairments such as hyperthermia, hypothermia

b5501.title: Maintenance of body temperature
b5501.description: Functions involved in maintaining optimal body temperature as environmental temperature changes.
inclusions: Impairments such as disrupted temperature regulation, heat and cold intolerance

b555.title: Endocrine gland functions
b555.description: Functions of production and regulation of hormonal levels in the body, including cyclical changes.
inclusions: Functions of hormonal balance; hyperpituitarism, hypopituitarism, hyperthyroidism, hypothyroidism, hyperadrenalism, hypoadrenalism; hyperparathyroidism, hypoparathyroidism; hypergonadism, hypogonadism

b559.title: Functions related to metabolism and the endocrine system, other specified and unspecified

b598.title: Functions of the digestive, metabolic and endocrine systems, other specified

b599.title: Functions of the digestive, metabolic and endocrine systems, unspecified

b610.title: Urinary excretory functions
b610.description: Functions of filtration and collection of the urine.
inclusions: Functions of filtration of urine, collection of urine; impairments such as renal insufficiency, anuria, oliguria, hydronephrosis, hypotonic urinary bladder and ureteral obstruction

b6100.title: Filtration of urine
b6100.description: Functions of filtration of urine by the kidneys.

b6101.title: Collection of urine
b6101.description: Functions of collection and storage of urine by the ureters and bladder.

b620.title: Urination functions
b620.description: Functions of discharge of urine from the urinary bladder.
inclusions: Functions of urination, frequency of urination, urinary continence; impairments such as stress incontinence, urge incontinence, reflex incontinence, overflow incontinence and continuous incontinence

b6200.title: Urination
b6200.description: Functions of voiding the urinary bladder.
inclusions: Impairments such as urinary retention

b6201.title: Frequency of urination
b6201.description: Functions involved in the number of times urination occurs.
inclusions: Impairments such as polyuria, oliguria and frequency

b6202.title: Urinary continence
inclusions: Impairments such as stress, urge, reflex, overflow, continuous urinary incontinence; dribbling

b630.title: Sensations associated with urinary functions
b630.description: Sensations arising from voiding and related urinary functions.
inclusions: Sensations of incomplete voiding of urine, feeling of fullness of bladder

b639.title: Urinary functions, other specified and unspecified

b640.title: Sexual functions
b640.description: Mental and physical functions related to the sexual act, including the arousal, preparatory, orgasmic and resolution stages.
inclusions: Functions of the sexual arousal, preparatory, orgasmic and resolution phase; functions related to sexual interest, performance, penile erection, clitoral erection, vaginal lubrication, ejaculation, orgasm; impairments such as impotence, frigidity, vaginismus, premature ejaculation, priapism and delayed ejaculation
exclusions: Sensations associated with genital and reproductive functions (b670)

b6400.title: Functions of sexual arousal phase
b6400.description: Functions of sexual interest and excitement.

b6401.title: Functions of sexual preparatory phase
b6401.description: Functions of engaging in sexual intercourse (e.g. penile erection, clitoral erection, vaginal lubrication).

b6403.title: Functions of sexual resolution phase
b6403.description: Functions of satisfaction after orgasm and accompanying relaxation.
inclusions: Impairments such as dissatisfaction with sexual resolution

b650.title: Menstruation functions
b650.description: Functions associated with the menstrual cycle, including regularity of menstruation and discharge of menstrual fluids.
inclusions: Functions of regularity and interval of menstruation, extent of menstrual bleeding, menarche, menopause; impairments such as primary and secondary amenorrhoea, menorrhagia, polymenorrhoea and retrograde menstruation
exclusions: Sensations associated with genital and reproductive functions (b670)

b6500.title: Regularity of menstrual cycle
b6500.description: Functions involved in the regularity of the menstrual cycle.
inclusions: Impairments such as irregular periods

b6501.title: Interval between menstruation
b6501.description: Functions related to the length of the menstrual cycle.
inclusions: Impairments such as too frequent periods (polymenorrhoea) or too infrequent periods (oligomenorrhoea)

b6502.title: Extent of menstrual bleeding
b6502.description: Functions involved in the amount of menstrual flow.
inclusions: Impairments such as menorrhagia (too much) and hypomenorrhoea (too little)

b660.title: Procreation functions
b660.description: Functions associated with fertility, pregnancy, childbirth and lactation.
inclusions: Functions of fertility and pregnancy; impairments such as subfertility, sterility, spontaneous abortions, ectopic pregnancy, miscarriage, small foetus, hydramnios and premature birth, delayed birth

b6600.title: Functions related to fertility
b6600.description: Functions related to the ability to produce gametes for procreation.
inclusions: Impairments such as subfertility and sterility

b6601.title: Functions related to pregnancy
b6601.description: Functions involved in becoming pregnant and being pregnant.

b6603.title: Lactation
b6603.description: Functions involved in the production of milk and making it available to the child.

b670.title: Sensations associated with genital and reproductive functions
b670.description: Sensations arising from sexual arousal, intercourse, menstruation and related genital or reproductive functions.
inclusions: Sensations of dyspareunia, dysmenorrhoea, hot flushes and night sweats during menopause

b6700.title: Discomfort associated with sexual intercourse
b6700.description: Sensations associated with sexual intercourse including dyspareunia.

b6701.title: Discomfort associated with the menstrual cycle
b6701.description: Sensations associated with menstruation, including dysmenorrhoea.

b6702.title: Discomfort associated with menopause
b6702.description: Sensations associated with cessation of the menstrual cycle, including hot flushes and night sweats.

b6708.title: Sensations associated with genital and reproductive functions, other specified

b6709.title: Sensations associated with genital and reproductive functions, unspecified

b679.title: Genital and reproductive functions, other specified and unspecified

b698.title: Genitourinary and reproductive functions, other specified

b710.title: Mobility of joint functions
b710.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of a joint.
inclusions: Functions of mobility of single or several joints, and of vertebral, shoulder, elbow, wrist, hip, knee, ankle, small joints of hands and feet; generalized joint mobility; impairments such as hypermobility of joints, frozen joints, frozen shoulder, arthritis

b7100.title: Mobility of a single joint
b7100.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of one joint.

b7101.title: Mobility of several joints
b7101.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of more than one joint.

b7102.title: Generalized joint mobility
b7102.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of joints throughout the body.
inclusions: Impairments such as generalized hypermobility of joints

b7108.title: Mobility of joint functions, other specified

b715.title: Stability of joint functions
b715.description: Functions of the maintenance of structural integrity of the joints.
inclusions: Functions of the stability of a single joint, several joints and joints in general; impairments such as unstable shoulder joint, dislocation of a joint, dislocation of the shoulder and hip

b7150.title: Stability of a single joint
b7150.description: Functions of the maintenance of structural integrity of one joint.

b7151.title: Stability of several joints
b7151.description: Functions of the maintenance of structural integrity of more than one joint.

b7152.title: Generalized joint stability
b7152.description: Functions of the maintenance of structural integrity of joints throughout the body.

b7158.title: Stability of joint functions, other specified

b720.title: Mobility of bone functions
b720.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of the scapula, pelvis, carpal and tarsal bones.
inclusions: Impairments such as frozen scapula and frozen pelvis

b7200.title: Mobility of scapula
b7200.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of the scapula.
inclusions: Impairments such as winged and frozen scapula

b7201.title: Mobility of pelvis
b7201.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of the pelvis.
inclusions: Impairments such as frozen pelvis

b7202.title: Mobility of carpal bones
b7202.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of the carpal bones.

b7203.title: Mobility of tarsal bones
b7203.description: Functions of the range and ease of movement of the tarsal bones.

b7208.title: Mobility of bone functions, other specified

b729.title: Functions of the joints and bones, other specified and unspecified

b730.title: Muscle power functions
b730.description: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of a muscle or muscle groups.
inclusions: Functions associated with the power of specific muscles and muscle groups, muscles of one limb, one side of the body, the lower half of the body, all limbs, the trunk and the body as a whole; impairments such as weakness of small muscles in feet and hands, muscle paresis, muscle paralysis, monoplegia, hemiplegia, paraplegia, quadriplegia and akinetic mutism

b7300.title: Power of isolated muscles and muscle groups
b7300.description: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of specific and isolated muscles and muscle groups.
inclusions: Impairments such as weakness of small muscles of thumbs or feet

b7301.title: Power of muscles of one limb
b7301.description: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of the muscles and muscle groups of one arm or one leg.
inclusions: Impairments such as monoparesis and monoplegia

b7302.title: Power of muscles of one side of the body
b7302.description: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of the muscles and muscle groups found on the left or right side of the body.
inclusions: Impairments such as hemiparesis and hemiplegia

b7303.title: Power of muscles of lower half of the body
b7303.description: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of the muscles and muscle groups found in the lower half of the body.
inclusions: Impairments such as paraparesis and paraplegia

b7304.title: Power of muscles of all limbs
b7304.description: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of the muscles and muscle groups of all four limbs.
inclusions: Impairments such as tetraparesis and tetraplegia

b7305.title: Power of muscles of the trunk
b7305.description: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of the muscles and muscle groups in the trunk.

b7306.title: Power of all muscles of the body
b7306.description: Functions related to the force generated by the contraction of all muscles and muscle groups of the body.
inclusions: Impairments such as in generalized myopathy and myasthenia

b735.title: Muscle tone functions
b735.description: Functions related to the tension present in the resting muscles and the resistance offered when trying to move the muscles passively.
inclusions: Functions associated with the tension of isolated muscles and muscle groups, muscles of one limb, one side of the body and the lower half of the body, muscles of all limbs, muscles of the trunk, and all muscles of the body; impairments such as hypotonia, hypertonia and muscle spasticity

b7350.title: Tone of isolated muscles and muscle groups
b7350.description: Functions related to the tension present in the resting isolated muscles and muscle groups.
inclusions: Impairments such as focal dystonia, e.g. torticollis

b7351.title: Tone of muscles of one limb
b7351.description: Functions related to the tension present in the resting muscles and muscle groups of one arm or leg.
inclusions: Impairments such as monoparesis and monoplegia

b7352.title: Tone of muscles of one side of the body
b7352.description: Functions related to the tension present in the resting muscles and muscle groups of the right or left side of the body.
inclusions: Impairments such as hemiparesis

b7353.title: Tone of muscles of lower half of the body
b7353.description: Functions related to the tension present in the resting muscles and muscle groups in the lower half of the body.
inclusions: Impairments such as paraparesis

b7354.title: Tone of muscles of all limbs
b7354.description: Functions related to the tension present in the resting muscles and muscle groups in all four limbs.
inclusions: Impairments such as tetraparesis

b7355.title: Tone of muscles of the trunk
b7355.description: Functions related to the tension present in the resting muscles and muscle groups of the trunk.

b7356.title: Tone of all muscles of the body
b7356.description: Functions related to the tension present in the resting muscles and muscle groups of the entire body.
inclusions: Impairments such as general hypotonia or hypertonia, as in cerebral palsy

b740.title: Muscle endurance functions
b740.description: Functions related to sustaining muscle contraction for the required period of time.
inclusions: Functions associated with sustaining muscle contraction for isolated muscles and muscle groups, and all muscles of the body; impairments such as in myasthenia gravis

b7400.title: Endurance of isolated muscles
b7400.description: Functions related to sustaining muscle contraction of isolated muscles for the required period of time.

b7401.title: Endurance of muscle groups
b7401.description: Functions related to sustaining muscle contraction of isolated muscle groups for the required period of time.

b7402.title: Endurance of all muscles of the body
b7402.description: Functions related to sustaining muscle contraction of all muscles of the body for the required period of time.
inclusions: Impairments such as in generalized myasthenia

b749.title: Muscle functions, other specified and unspecified

b750.title: Motor reflex functions
b750.description: Functions of involuntary contraction of muscles automatically induced by specific stimuli.
inclusions: Functions of stretch motor reflex, automatic local joint reflex, reflexes generated by noxious stimuli and other exteroceptive stimuli; withdrawal reflex, biceps reflex, radius reflex, quadriceps reflex, patellar reflex, ankle reflex

b7500.title: Stretch motor reflex
b7500.description: Functions of involuntary contractions of muscles automatically induced by stretching.
inclusions: Impairments such as hyperreflexia and hyporeflexia

b7501.title: Reflexes generated by noxious stimuli
b7501.description: Functions of involuntary contractions of muscles automatically induced by painful or other noxious stimuli.
inclusions: Impairments such as flexor withdrawal

b7502.title: Reflexes generated by other exteroceptive stimuli
b7502.description: Functions of involuntary contractions of muscles automatically induced by external stimuli other than noxious stimuli.

b755.title: Involuntary movement reaction functions
b755.description: Functions of involuntary contractions of large muscles or of the whole body induced by body position, balance and threatening stimuli.
inclusions: Functions of postural reactions, righting reactions, body adjustment reactions, balance reactions, supporting reactions, defensive reactions

b760.title: Control of voluntary movement functions
b760.description: Functions associated with control over and coordination of voluntary movements.
inclusions: Functions of control of simple voluntary movements and of complex voluntary movements, coordination of voluntary movements, supportive functions of arm or leg, right-left motor coordination, eye-hand coordination, eye-foot coordination
Impairments such as control and coordination problems, e.g. dysdiadochokinesia

b7600.title: Control of simple voluntary movements
b7600.description: Functions associated with control over and coordination of simple or isolated voluntary movements.

b7601.title: Control of complex voluntary movements
b7601.description: Functions associated with control over and coordination of complex voluntary movements.

b7602.title: Coordination of voluntary movements
b7602.description: Functions associated with coordination of simple and complex voluntary movements, performing movements in an orderly combination.
inclusions: Right-left coordination, coordination of visually directed movements, e.g. eye-hand coordination and eye-foot coordination; impairments such as dysdiadochokinesia

b7603.title: Supportive functions of arm or leg
b7603.description: Functions associated with control over and coordination of voluntary movements, by putting weight on arms (elbows and hands) or legs (knees and feet) and sustaining it.

b7608.title: Control of voluntary movement functions, other specified

b7609.title: Control of voluntary movement functions, unspecified

b765.title: Involuntary movement functions
b765.description: Functions of unintentional, non- or semi-purposive involuntary contractions of a muscle or group of muscles.
inclusions: Involuntary contractions of muscles; impairments such as tremors, tics, mannerisms, stereotypies, motor perseveration, chorea, athetosis, vocal tics, dystonic movements and dyskinesia

b7650.title: Involuntary contractions of muscles
b7650.description: Functions of unintentional, non- or semi-purposive involuntary contractions of a muscle or a group of muscles, such as those involved in part of a pathological movement syndrome.
inclusions: Impairments such as chorea and athetosis; dystonic movements, as in torticollis or dystonia and dyskinesia

b7651.title: Tremor
b7651.description: Functions of alternating contraction and relaxation of a group of muscles around a joint, resulting in shakiness.
inclusions: Impairments such as resting, action, intention, postural and flapping tremors

b7652.title: Tics and mannerisms
b7652.description: Functions of repetitive, quasi-purposive, involuntary contractions of a group of muscles.
inclusions: Impairments such as vocal tics, motor tics, coprolalia

b7653.title: Stereotypies and motor perseveration
b7653.description: Functions of sustained, seemingly purposeless involuntary movements, such as rocking, nodding, drumming and repetitive purposeless movements.

b770.title: Gait pattern functions
b770.description: Functions of movement patterns associated with walking, running or other whole body movements.
inclusions: Walking patterns and running patterns; impairments such as spastic gait, hemiplegic gait, paraplegic gait, asymmetric gait, limping and stiff gait pattern

b780.title: Sensations related to muscles and movement functions
b780.description: Sensations associated with the muscles or muscle groups of the body and their movement.
inclusions: Sensations of muscle stiffness, tightness of muscles, muscle spasm or contracture, and heaviness of muscles

b7800.title: Sensation of muscle stiffness
b7800.description: Sensation of tightness or stiffness of muscles.

b7801.title: Sensation of muscle spasm
b7801.description: Sensation of involuntary contraction of a muscle or a group of muscles.

b7808.title: Sensations related to muscles and movement functions, other specified

b7809.title: Sensations related to muscles and movement functions, unspecified

b789.title: Movement functions, other specified and unspecified

b798.title: Neuromusculoskeletal and movement related functions, other specified

b799.title: Neuromusculoskeletal and movement related functions, unspecified

b810.title: Protective functions of the skin
b810.description: Functions of the skin for protecting the body from physical, chemical and biological threats.
inclusions: Functions of protecting against the sun and other radiation, photosensitivity, pigmentation, quality of the skin; insulating function of skin, callus formation, hardening; impairments such as breakdown of skin, ulcers, bedsores and thinning of skin

b820.title: Repair functions of the skin
b820.description: Functions of the skin for repairing defects and other damage to the skin.
inclusions: Functions of scab formation, healing, scarring; bruising and keloid formation

b830.title: Other functions of the skin
b830.description: Functions of the skin other than protection and repair, such as cooling and sweat secretion.
inclusions: Functions of sweating, glandular functions of the skin and resulting body odour

b840.title: Sensation related to the skin
b840.description: Sensations related to the skin such as itching, burning sensation and tingling.
inclusions: Sensations of tingling, feeling of a formication, and sensation of pins and needles; impairments such as burning sensation, itching

b849.title: Functions of the skin, other specified and unspecified

b850.title: Functions of hair
b850.description: Functions of hair, such as protection, colouration and appearance.
inclusions: Functions of growth of hair, pigmentation of hair, location of hair; impairments such as loss of hair, alopecia

b860.title: Functions of nails
b860.description: Functions of nails, such as protection, scratching and appearance.
inclusions: Growth, pigmentation, shape of nails

b869.title: Functions of hair and nails, other specified and unspecified

b898.title: Functions of the skin and related structures, other specified

b899.title: Functions of the skin and related structures, unspecified

s199.title: Structure of the nervous system, unspecified

s299.title: Eye, ear and related structures, unspecified

s399.title: Structures involved in voice and speech, unspecified

s499.title: Structure of the cardiovascular, immunological and respiratory systems, unspecified

s598.title: Structures related to the digestive, metabolic and endocrine systems, other specified

s599.title: Structures related to the digestive, metabolic and endocrine systems, unspecified

s699.title: Structures related to genitourinary and reproductive systems, unspecified
